document.name: Medical Necessity Criteria – Biologic Therapy for Severe Asthma

medical_necessity_criteria.description: Biologic therapy may be considered medically necessary when ALL of the following criteria are met.

criteria.description: Member has a confirmed diagnosis of severe persistent asthma
Symptoms remain uncontrolled despite high-dose inhaled corticosteroids AND long-acting beta-agonist therapy
Evidence of frequent exacerbations (≥2 in the prior 12 months)
criteria.exacerbation_definition: Emergency department visit, hospitalization, or course of systemic corticosteroids for asthma

criteria.description: Prescribing provider is a pulmonologist or allergist

approved_biologics.indication: Moderate-to-severe allergic asthma with elevated IgE
Moderate-to-severe eosinophilic asthma or oral corticosteroid-dependent asthma
Severe asthma with no phenotype limitation

prior_authorization.applies_to: All biologic therapies addressed under this policy
prior_authorization.renewal_criteria: Documented clinical benefit and continued medical necessity
submission_requirements: Baseline lab values (IgE, eosinophil count as applicable)
